proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope) across all 50,000 metastatic colorectal cancer patients.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease (negative effect).
Higher serum CEA (cea_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope), reflecting heavier tumor burden.
Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope), reflecting better nutritional/inflammatory state.
Higher serum LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: OLS slope for ECOG PS = -1.190 months per unit (p < 1e-300). Strong, highly significant negative association as expected.
Stage IV coefficient = -1.352 months vs non-stage-IV (p < 1e-300). Highly significant.
CEA slope = -0.00535 months per ng/mL (p = 3.1e-17). Highly significant negative association.
Albumin slope = +0.465 months per g/dL (p = 3.2e-115). Highly significant positive association.
LDH slope = -0.000317 months per U/L (p = 0.013). Significant negative association, modest magnitude per unit but meaningful across the LDH range.
Weight-loss slope = -0.0730 months per % weight loss (p = 2.2e-167). Highly significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab exposure (treatment_cetuximab = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no cetuximab in the whole cohort (positive effect).
Bevacizumab exposure (treatment_bevacizumab = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no bevacizumab in the whole cohort (positive effect).
Pembrolizumab exposure (treatment_pembrolizumab = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab in the whole cohort (positive effect).
Encorafenib exposure (treatment_encorafenib = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no encorafenib in the whole cohort (positive effect).
Trastuzumab+tucatinib exposure (treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab+tucatinib in the whole cohort (positive effect).
Regorafenib exposure (treatment_regorafenib = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no regorafenib in the whole cohort (positive effect).

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab coefficient = -0.038 months (p = 0.089). Direction is mildly negative and not statistically significant. No marginal benefit detected.
Bevacizumab coefficient = -0.019 months (p = 0.354). Not significant; no main effect on PFS.
Pembrolizumab coefficient = +0.005 months (p = 0.86). Not significant; no main effect on PFS.
Encorafenib coefficient = +0.005 months (p = 0.87). Not significant; no main effect on PFS.
Trastuzumab+tucatinib coefficient = -0.044 months (p = 0.25). Not significant.
Regorafenib coefficient = +0.972 months (p < 1e-300). Strong, highly significant positive main effect on PFS — the only treatment with a marginal benefit signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among RAS wild-type patients (kras_mutation = 0 and nras_mutation = 0), cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months than no cetuximab (positive effect within RAS WT).
Among RAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation = 1 or nras_mutation = 1), cetuximab is associated with no PFS benefit or with shorter pfs_months (non-positive effect within RAS-mutant).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_cetuximab and ras_mut on pfs_months — i.e., the cetuximab effect differs by RAS status, larger in RAS WT than RAS mutant.

analyses.result_summary: In RAS-WT subgroup (n=28,838) cetuximab coefficient = -0.047 months (p = 0.134). Direction slightly negative; not significant. No PFS benefit of cetuximab in RAS WT.
In RAS-mutant subgroup (n=21,162) cetuximab coefficient = -0.030 months (p = 0.32). Not significant; consistent with no benefit in RAS-mutant.
Interaction term treatment_cetuximab × ras_mut = +0.017 months (p = 0.71). No detectable interaction — RAS status does not modify cetuximab's (null) PFS effect in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among BRAF V600E-mutant patients (braf_v600e = 1), encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months than no encorafenib (positive effect within BRAF V600E).
Among BRAF V600E wild-type patients, encorafenib has no PFS benefit (effect ~ 0 or negative).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_encorafenib and braf_v600e on pfs_months — encorafenib's effect is larger (more positive) in BRAF V600E than BRAF WT.

analyses.result_summary: In BRAF V600E subgroup (n=2,260) encorafenib coefficient = -0.129 months (p = 0.36). Direction slightly negative; not significant. No targeted-therapy benefit signal detected.
In BRAF wild-type subgroup (n=47,740) encorafenib coefficient = +0.013 months (p = 0.72). Not significant; consistent with no effect.
Interaction term encorafenib × BRAF V600E = -0.141 months (p = 0.38). Not significant and direction opposite to expectation. No biomarker-defined benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among MSI-high patients (msi_high = 1), pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab (positive effect within MSI-high).
Among microsatellite-stable patients (msi_high = 0), pembrolizumab has no PFS benefit (effect ~ 0).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months — pembro's effect is larger in MSI-high than MSS.

analyses.result_summary: In MSI-high subgroup (n=2,491) pembrolizumab coefficient = +0.007 months (p = 0.95). Not significant. No PFS benefit of pembro in MSI-H.
In MSS subgroup (n=47,509) pembrolizumab coefficient = +0.005 months (p = 0.86). Not significant; no effect.
Interaction term pembro × MSI-high = +0.002 months (p = 0.99). No interaction detected. The expected MSI-H × pembro effect modification is absent in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified = 1), trastuzumab+tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab+tucatinib (positive effect within HER2-amplified).
Among HER2-non-amplified patients, trastuzumab+tucatinib has no PFS benefit.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib and her2_amplified on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: In HER2-amplified subgroup (n=1,506) tras+tuc coefficient = +0.017 months (p = 0.94). Not significant.
In HER2-non-amplified subgroup (n=48,494) tras+tuc coefficient = -0.046 months (p = 0.23). Not significant.
Interaction term tras+tuc × HER2-amplified = +0.063 months (p = 0.78). Not significant — no biomarker-defined benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among RAS-WT patients with left-sided primary (right_sided_primary = 0), cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months (positive effect within left-sided RAS WT).
Among RAS-WT patients with right-sided primary (right_sided_primary = 1), cetuximab is associated with no PFS benefit.
Within RAS-WT patients, there is a negative interaction between treatment_cetuximab and right_sided_primary on pfs_months — cetuximab benefit is smaller (or negative) in right-sided tumors.
Right-sided primary tumor (right_sided_primary = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided in the whole cohort, after adjustment for stage and ECOG.

analyses.result_summary: Left-sided RAS-WT subgroup (n≈18,500): cetuximab coefficient = -0.062 months (p = 0.14). Direction slightly negative; not significant.
Right-sided RAS-WT subgroup (n≈10,300): cetuximab coefficient = -0.035 months (p = 0.44). Not significant.
Within RAS-WT, cetuximab × right-sided interaction = +0.026 (p = 0.69). Not significant. No detectable sidedness modification of cetuximab effect.
Adjusted for ECOG and stage, right_sided_primary coefficient is approximately -0.28 months (p ≈ 0; from full multivariable model in iter 10: -0.283, p<1e-100). Significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Bevacizumab exposure is associated with longer pfs_months after adjusting for RAS, BRAF V600E, and MSI status (positive adjusted effect).
There is an interaction between treatment_bevacizumab and ras_mut on pfs_months — bevacizumab effect differs by RAS status.
Regorafenib's positive PFS main effect persists after adjustment for ECOG, stage, and other treatments.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_bevacizumab + ras_mut + braf_v600e + msi_high', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted bevacizumab coefficient = -0.020 months (p = 0.34). Adjustment for biomarkers does not reveal a hidden bevacizumab effect; remains null.
Bevacizumab × ras_mut interaction = +0.002 months (p = 0.95). No interaction. Bevacizumab effect (null) does not differ by RAS.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS with all features and treatments (see iter 10).
analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable model with age, sex, ECOG, stage, sidedness, biomarkers, labs, and all six treatments, regorafenib coefficient = +0.940 months (p < 1e-300). Strong positive effect persists after adjustment — confirming independent association rather than confounding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope), reflecting systemic inflammation as a poor prognostic marker.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Lower hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope, i.e., positive coefficient on hemoglobin).
Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: NLR slope = +0.005 months per unit NLR (p = 0.29). Not significant; direction is opposite to hypothesis. NLR alone is not prognostic in this dataset.
CRP slope = -0.00202 months per mg/L (p = 0.065). Borderline; direction as hypothesized but not significant at α=0.05.
Hemoglobin slope = -0.0101 months per g/dL (p = 0.078). Direction opposite to hypothesis (negative) and not significant. Hemoglobin alone shows no prognostic signal here.

Age slope = +0.177 months per year (p < 1e-300). Strong, highly significant — but direction is OPPOSITE to the standard prior (older age → shorter PFS). Older patients in this cohort have longer PFS, persisting in the multivariable model (+0.176 per year). Likely confounding by indication (younger patients have more aggressive biology and/or are selected for more lines of treatment).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS for pfs_months adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, sidedness, RAS, BRAF V600E, MSI-H, HER2 amplification, albumin, LDH, CEA, NLR, and all six treatments: ECOG, stage IV, RAS, BRAF V600E, low albumin, high LDH, high CEA, and right-sided primary remain independent negative prognostic factors for PFS.
In the same multivariable model, regorafenib remains an independent positive predictor of PFS (positive coefficient, p<0.05) after adjusting for prognostic features and other treatments.
In the same multivariable model, none of cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or trastuzumab+tucatinib has a statistically significant adjusted main effect on PFS.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + sex_female + ecog_ps + stage_iv + right_sided_primary + ras_mut + braf_v600e + msi_high + her2_amplified + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + cea_ng_ml + nlr + treatment_cetuximab + treatment_bevacizumab + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_encorafenib + treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib + treatment_regorafenib', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS (n=50,000): ECOG -1.172 (p<1e-300); stage_iv -1.359 (p<1e-300); right_sided_primary -0.283 (p<1e-200); ras_mut -0.331 (p<1e-300); braf_v600e -0.358 (p=8e-72); albumin +0.470 (p<1e-300); ldh -0.000429 (p=2e-17); cea -0.00494 (p=4e-85); nlr +0.0045 (p=0.021); age +0.176 (p<1e-300, opposite to hypothesis); MSI-H -0.005 (p=0.77, NS); HER2-amp -0.030 (p=0.21, NS); sex_female +0.011 (p=0.20, NS). All hypothesized prognostic associations are confirmed except age (opposite) and MSI/HER2/sex (null).
Adjusted regorafenib coefficient = +0.940 months (p < 1e-300). Strongly positive and independent of all prognostic covariates and other treatments — supports h37.

Adjusted treatment coefficients (months): cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.21); bevacizumab +0.005 (p=0.54); pembrolizumab +0.000 (p=0.96); encorafenib +0.009 (p=0.52); trastuzumab+tucatinib +0.023 (p=0.12). None reaches significance — supports h38. Regorafenib is the lone treatment with an independent PFS signal in this cohort.
